subject: Re: ts_aretomo "all tilt angles are zero"
body:
Hi Lucas,

Sorry, this ended up in spam... unfortunately this is a known issue with
AreTomo2 - we've reverted to using AreTomo v1.3.4 from our side...

We reported the error but it has not been fixed in AreTomo2:
https://github.com/czimaginginstitute/AreTomo2/issues/21

AreTomo3 prior to version 2 which was released this week could not be run
on tilt stacks - this has now changed so I opened an issue to track
updating our wrapper for AreTomo3 compatibility a few days ago:
https://github.com/warpem/warp/issues/279

Sorry for the mess but it's unfortunately out of our control

Cheers,

Alister

On Mon, 21 Oct 2024 at 09:15, Lucas Morley <lmorley1100@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi,
>
> I am trying to use AreTomo to align a tilt stack with very few fiducials.
> I have run the command:
>
> WarpTools ts_aretomo --settings warp_tiltseries2.settings --angpix 10
> --alignz 3500 --input_data tomostar2/sm2020-01-25-19_TS6.tomostar
>
> This seems to run fine until the import step which I get the error:
>
> Failed to import alignments:
> all tilt angles are zero in
> redacted/warp_tiltseries2/tiltstack/sm2020-01-25-19_TS6/sm2020-01-25-19_TS6_Imod/sm2020-01-25-19_TS6_st.tlt
>
> It appears AreTomo ran fine. The aligned mrc looks great, but the .st in
> the Imod directory has all 0's as the error says.
>
> I am unsure if this is an AreTomo issue or Warp issue.
>
> Thanks for any help.
>
> --

> "Warp" group.

> .
>
